hard.next_step: complete RFP. Why not Zycus: they don't use MS Teams, they use Google Chat which per Bikash is not on the roadmap, which removes one of our biggest advantages. They have 1,800 eProc users today and are looking to grow. They already use Basware and Coupa. Buyer response: primary driver is global consolidation following the merger; want to explore market developments and AI tools for an agile global solution. Challenges: long tail of countries, local compliance vs easy global process, global budget visibility, supplier pushback, ease of configurations. Will reevaluate whether we stay in the deal after next discussion.
hard.ais_why: Strong buyer-stated AI signal in the next-step note ('explore the latest market developments and AI tools to find an agile, global solution') set against a Google Chat / MS Teams fit gap and two entrenched incumbents (Basware, Coupa) across 1,800 eProc users; zero Avoma calls to corroborate.

north_star_verdict.headline: Teads has gone silent since acknowledging our RFP response on 01 Apr; Edward's 04 Jun and 09 Jun chasers drew no reply, and the rep's own note questions whether Zycus should stay in a five-vendor RFP where Google Chat (not MS Teams) removes a core differentiator and Basware plus Coupa are entrenched across 1,800 users.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 06 Mar 2026, 122 days old. In Formal Evaluation since 26 Mar (no verified submission-date field). Close date 31 Dec 2026, 178 days out. Last genuine buyer inbound was the 01 Apr RFP-receipt acknowledgment (plus a 13 May out-of-office auto-reply); Edward's 07 May, 04 Jun and 09 Jun follow-ups went unanswered, so roughly 3 months without two-way buyer engagement. Cold beyond the deal's cadence puts it Off Track.
evidence: Last two-way buyer contact was Anush Jeunet's 01 Apr RFP-receipt acknowledgment; a 13 May reply was an out-of-office auto-reply, and Edward's 04 Jun and 09 Jun chasers got no response (SF Tasks).
Rep's own Next Step note records internal doubt: 'will need to reevaluate whether we stay in the deal or not after next discussion... and have them explain why Zycus.'
Google Chat vs MS Teams fit gap: 'They use Google chat which according to Bikash is not on the roadmap. This takes away one of our biggest advantages' (Next Step).
Two incumbents already deployed: 'They already use Basware and Coupa' with 1,800 eProc users today (Next Step), a high displacement bar.
Five-vendor RFP (Basware, Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER) per the Competitor field, no competitive-intel refresh since 05 Jun.
Single contact role (Tamar Avni, SVP AI & Information System Engineering); no Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion mapped.
Buyer's stated driver is post-merger global consolidation plus AI tools, long-tail country compliance, global budget visibility and supplier pushback (Next Step, buyer response).
Avoma fully exhausted across opportunity, account, teads.tv, teads.com and outbrain.com; zero buyer calls found.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (buyer response, last modified 05 Jun 2026)
SF Products__c (1,800 eProc users referenced in Next Step)
metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated operational pain in their RFP response captured in the Next Step (early Jun 2026): managing a long tail of countries post-merger, holding local compliance while keeping an easy global process, weak global budget visibility, supplier pushback, and configuration rigidity in their current stack. None of it is quantified: no cost of compliance failures, no budget-variance figure, no cycle-time or savings target, and the 1,800 eProc user base is only scale context, not a dollar value. Without a number the deal has no financial anchor for an economic-buyer conversation. The move is to convert these qualitative pains into a savings and efficiency model, but that requires re-opening a buyer dialogue that has been silent since 01 Apr.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (only Tamar Avni, no EB role)
SF Tasks (Anush Jeunet, procurement point of contact)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is mapped. The only opportunity contact role is Tamar Avni (SVP AI & Information System Engineering), a technical executive, not a stated budget owner. Anush Jeunet surfaces in the email trail as the buyer's procurement point of contact ('I'm your main point of contact. Mimi left the company', 01 Apr) after Mimi Ji departed on 31 Mar, but nothing shows Anush controls the P2P budget, and the emails never name who signs. For a $397k post-merger consolidation the budget owner is likely in Finance, but no such person is verified in Salesforce, so promoting a name here would be a guess. The move is to ask Anush directly who owns the consolidation budget and secure an introduction; identifying the economic buyer is a ranked next step.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer framed selection around post-merger global consolidation and named concrete criteria in their RFP response: an agile global solution, latest market developments and AI tooling, handling a long tail of countries, local compliance alongside an easy global process, global budget visibility, easing supplier pushback, and ease of configuration (Next Step). These are buyer-stated and confirmed as evaluation drivers, run through a formal RFP that Zycus answered on 01 Apr. What is not confirmed is the scoring weight, the finalist bar, or whether the Google Chat collaboration requirement is a hard gate: the rep flags it as a lost advantage but the buyer has not said it is disqualifying. The move is to get the buyer to explain their weighting and confirm whether the Teams/Google Chat gap eliminates us.

sources: SF Field history (Qualified to Formal Evaluation, 26 Mar)
decision_process.narrative: The process is a formal, procurement-led RFP: an NDA and full RFP package were exchanged in early March, a question round closed 16 Mar, the submission deadline was extended, and Zycus submitted on 31 Mar / 01 Apr, at which point the buyer confirmed 'We are now entering the evaluation.' Procurement (Anush Jeunet) runs the intake and is the routing point after Mimi Ji left on 31 Mar. What is unknown is the evaluation calendar, the shortlisting mechanism, and the approval chain beyond procurement, and the buyer has given no timeline since 01 Apr. The move is to get the written evaluation timeline and the decision milestones from Anush before assuming any pace.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are effectively unknown at this early stage. The only paper touched so far is the NDA handled around 09 Mar and the RFP response package; there is no MSA, SOW, order form, legal-review, procurement-portal or InfoSec activity because the deal has not reached vendor selection. No contract-expiration forcing function is recorded for the incumbent Basware/Coupa environment. This is an appropriate blank for a Formal Evaluation deal and not a risk yet; visibility will come only once Teads shortlists and moves to commercials. The near-term move is not paper, it is re-establishing a live buyer conversation.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is post-merger (Teads/Outbrain) procurement complexity: managing a long tail of countries with disparate local compliance, no global budget visibility, supplier pushback, and configuration rigidity in the current Basware/Coupa stack (Next Step, buyer response). It is operationally specific and buyer-confirmed, and the merger is a genuine compelling event driving consolidation. What is missing is a financial consequence: no stated cost, no 'what if this is not done' impact, and no named individual owning the pain beyond the procurement function that Anush Jeunet routes. The move is to quantify the cost of the fragmented country-by-country model and attach it to the consolidation mandate.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Tamar Avni, no advocacy evidence)
champion.narrative: No champion exists. Tamar Avni (SVP AI & Information System Engineering) is the sole contact role and her AI-systems remit could align with the Merlin story, but there is zero evidence of advocacy: no introductions, no internal selling, no competitive intel shared, and she is not quoted anywhere. Anush Jeunet is a procurement point of contact and gatekeeper, not an advocate, and has gone quiet since 01 Apr. In a five-vendor RFP against two incumbents, having no one selling Zycus internally is the single biggest structural weakness of this deal. The move is to test whether Tamar will engage on the AI narrative and, in parallel, find the consolidation business owner who benefits most from displacing the fragmented stack.

sources: SF Competitors__c (Basware, Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, JAGGAER)
SF Next_Step__c (Basware/Coupa incumbents, Google Chat gap, internal doubt)
competition.narrative: Five named rivals sit in a formal RFP: Basware and Coupa are incumbents actively deployed to 1,800 eProc users ('They already use Basware and Coupa', Next Step), giving them switching-cost and familiarity leverage that Zycus must overcome, not merely match. GEP Worldwide is the strongest peer-suite threat (large-enterprise P2P plus AI and global compliance depth), with iValua and JAGGAER credible but a step behind on current signal. The rep flags a structural weakness that no competitor may share: Teads runs Google Chat, not MS Teams, and Google Chat is not on the Zycus roadmap per Bikash, removing a collaboration differentiator, and the rep's own note questions staying in the deal. The wedge, if viable, is Merlin AI for post-merger consolidation, long-tail country compliance automation and budget visibility where Basware and Coupa are weaker; but it is untested because there has been no buyer call and no competitive-intel refresh since 05 Jun.

deal_movement.summary: The deal advanced through the RFP mechanically (NDA 09 Mar, questions 16 Mar, submission 31 Mar/01 Apr, stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation 26 Mar, amount raised 300k to 397k on 26 Mar) but has flat-lined since the 01 Apr buyer acknowledgment: Edward's 07 May, 04 Jun and 09 Jun chasers drew no reply, and a key contact (Mimi Ji) left on 31 Mar.

items.change: Zycus submitted the full P2P RFP response; buyer confirmed receipt and 'entering the evaluation'
Buyer contact Mimi Ji left Teads; Anush Jeunet confirmed as main point of contact, Veronica Gonzalez named as backup
Stage moved Qualified to Formal Evaluation; amount raised 300,000 to 397,440
Edward's follow-up chasers on the RFP response went unanswered (07 May, 04 Jun, 09 Jun)

competitive_position.summary: Teads is running a five-vendor RFP with two entrenched incumbents. Basware and Coupa are the highest current threats: both are deployed today to 1,800 eProc users ('They already use Basware and Coupa', Next Step 05 Jun), so Zycus must justify displacement, not parity. GEP Worldwide is the strongest peer-suite challenger; iValua and JAGGAER are credible but lower on current signal. Zycus carries a structural handicap the incumbents may not: the buyer runs Google Chat, which is not on the Zycus roadmap per Bikash, removing a collaboration advantage, and the rep openly questions staying in. No competitive-intel refresh exists since 05 Jun and no buyer call has ever been read, so all threat levels are carried forward, not re-scored.

competitors.quote: They already use Basware and Coupa.
competitors.how_we_win: Prove Merlin AI and global compliance agility beat Basware for post-merger long-tail country management and quantify switching ROI to overcome deployment inertia.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: They already use Basware and Coupa.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Merlin AI and ease of configuration against Coupa's complexity and answer the supplier-pushback pain with iSupplier UX.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.how_we_win: Benchmark Merlin negotiation and intake agents against GEP Smart and lead with post-merger consolidation references.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: Position faster deployment and agility versus iValua's heavyweight suite, matching the buyer's stated ease-of-configuration criterion.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin AI depth and global compliance and out-reference JAGGAER on multinational consolidation.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Teads set clear consolidation-era criteria in the RFP; Zycus aligns on suite breadth and AI but is exposed on the Google Chat collaboration requirement and on displacing two incumbents.

best_practice_check.summary: This early-stage RFP has gone silent with no champion, no economic buyer, and a live incumbent-displacement fight; the win-strategy priority is to re-open a real buyer conversation and build internal advocacy before the evaluation closes without us.
flags: Re-open a live buyer dialogue: three months of one-way chasing means the next move is a value-led reason for Anush Jeunet to meet, not another status ping.
Build a champion and multi-thread beyond procurement: test Tamar Avni on the Merlin AI story and find the consolidation business owner who gains most from displacing Basware/Coupa.
Confront the Google Chat fit gap head-on: get the buyer to confirm whether it is disqualifying, and if not, reposition Merlin AI and global-compliance agility as the wedge against the two incumbents.

items.title: SVP AI & Information System Engineering

items.name: Anush Jeunet
items.risk: Procurement point of contact and RFP gatekeeper after Mimi Ji left on 31 Mar; has not replied to follow-ups since 01 Apr, so the routing thread has gone quiet.
items.source: SF Tasks (email trail, 01 Apr and 04/09 Jun)

champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Tamar Avni (SVP AI, sole contact role) shows no advocacy, no introductions and no competitive-intel sharing; Anush Jeunet is a procurement gatekeeper, not an advocate. In a five-vendor RFP against two incumbents, the absence of anyone selling Zycus internally is the deal's central weakness.
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Tamar Avni), SF Tasks (Anush Jeunet), SF Next_Step__c

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin and Merlin Intake are in scope and the buyer explicitly asked for 'the latest market developments and AI tools', but no specific Merlin or agentic ask is recorded and no buyer call has surfaced to land the story, so the AI narrative is scoped but under-delivered.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 5.0 / Moderate. The buyer voiced a genuine AI signal ('explore the latest market developments and AI tools to find an agile, global solution', Next Step), but the Google Chat/Teams fit gap and the absence of any Merlin-specific ask or buyer call cap the upside; no call evidence exists to show it under- or over-rated.

items.detail: Teads runs Google Chat, not MS Teams, and Google Chat is not on the Zycus roadmap per Bikash, removing a collaboration differentiator.
Two incumbents (Basware, Coupa) are deployed to 1,800 eProc users today, a high switching-cost and displacement bar.
No economic buyer or decision maker mapped; only a technical SVP is a contact role on a $397k post-merger consolidation deal.
Single-threaded through procurement; the sole advocate-capable contact (Tamar Avni) is unengaged and the routing contact has gone silent.
No buyer engagement since the 01 Apr RFP acknowledgment; evaluation could close without Zycus in the conversation.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has cooled to cold: the last two-way buyer contact was 01 Apr and three subsequent Zycus follow-ups (07 May, 04 Jun, 09 Jun) went unanswered, and the rep's own note questions staying in the deal.
items: Zero buyer replies to 07 May, 04 Jun and 09 Jun follow-ups (SF Tasks).
Rep-recorded internal doubt: 'will need to reevaluate whether we stay in the deal' (Next Step).
Key contact Mimi Ji departed 31 Mar, disrupting the thread.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A $397k five-vendor RFP that has gone cold: Teads has not replied since acknowledging our 01 Apr submission, Zycus has no champion and no economic buyer mapped, and two incumbents plus a Google Chat fit gap make displacement hard, so the one thing between us and re-entering the race is getting Anush Jeunet back to the table with a reason to choose Zycus.

win_position.text: Win position is weak and slipping: Teads has been silent since the 01 Apr RFP acknowledgment, we have no champion selling Zycus, and no economic buyer is mapped against two entrenched incumbents (Next Step, SF Tasks).
The Google Chat vs MS Teams gap removes a stated advantage: 'according to Bikash [Google Chat] is not on the roadmap. This takes away one of our biggest advantages' (Next Step, 05 Jun).
Basware and Coupa are already deployed to 1,800 eProc users, so Zycus must justify displacement, not parity, and no competitive-intel refresh exists since 05 Jun.
The one genuine opening is a real compelling event: post-merger global consolidation with a buyer-stated appetite for AI tooling that Merlin could serve if we get back in front of them (Next Step).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has stalled: after submitting on 01 Apr, Edward's 07 May, 04 Jun and 09 Jun chasers all went unanswered (SF Tasks).
No customer-named dates, no next meeting requested, and no senior buyer has joined since evaluation began (SF Tasks, no Avoma calls).

customer_commitment.text: The buyer's only commitment is procedural: they confirmed 'entering the evaluation' on 01 Apr and have committed to nothing Zycus-specific since (SF Task 01 Apr).
The rep's own note shows the relationship is unproven: the plan is to 'have them explain why Zycus,' meaning the buyer has given no signal of preference (Next Step).

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded through a procurement gatekeeper (Anush Jeunet) who has gone quiet, with the only executive contact (Tamar Avni, SVP AI) unengaged (SF OpportunityContactRole, Tasks).
A five-vendor field with two incumbents and a live collaboration fit gap makes this a high-loss-probability RFP unless a champion and economic buyer are found fast.

champion.evidence: No champion; sole contact role Tamar Avni shows no advocacy and Anush Jeunet is a gatekeeper (SF OpportunityContactRole, Tasks).

engagement.evidence: No buyer reply since 01 Apr across three follow-ups (SF Tasks).

competitive.evidence: Two entrenched incumbents plus a Google Chat fit gap versus a five-vendor field (Next Step).

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer response is neutral and process-driven ('explore the latest market developments and AI tools'); no Zycus preference expressed, and the rep's note plans to 'have them explain why Zycus' (Next Step, 05 Jun).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative post-merger consolidation pain articulated (long-tail country compliance, budget visibility, supplier pushback) but no cost, savings, or ROI quantified (Next Step).

scope_change.detail: Amount was raised from 300k to 397,440 on 26 Mar with a broad seven-product suite (iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake); scope has grown, not narrowed.

items.action: Send Anush Jeunet a consolidation value hook and request a 30-minute why-Zycus review.
items.trigger: Three unanswered follow-ups since the 01 Apr RFP acknowledgment; buyer silent for ~3 months.
items.expected_effect: Re-opens the dialogue and tests whether Zycus is still in the evaluation, the precondition for any progress.

items.action: Engage Tamar Avni on the Merlin AI and global-consolidation story to test her as a champion.
items.trigger: Sole contact role is SVP AI whose remit fits Merlin, but she is unengaged and no champion exists.
items.expected_effect: Creates internal advocacy and a second thread beyond a silent procurement gatekeeper.

items.action: Ask Anush who owns the global consolidation budget and request an introduction.
items.trigger: No economic buyer or decision maker mapped on a $397k deal.
items.expected_effect: Maps the budget owner so the business case reaches the person who signs.

items.action: Run an internal competitive review on the Google Chat gap and incumbent displacement.
items.trigger: Five-vendor RFP with two incumbents and a fit gap, no competitive refresh since 05 Jun.
items.expected_effect: Decides whether to invest or disqualify, and sharpens the Merlin wedge if we stay in.
